mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator that simplifies the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It is particularly useful for users looking to run distributed machine learning workloads efficiently. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts and potential complexity in managing Ray applications.

steps.title: Add the KubeRay Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository to your local Helm installation, allowing you to install KubeRay.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command registers the KubeRay repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeRay to manage Ray resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayjob.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayservice.yaml

```

> Installs the RayCluster CRD.
> Installs the RayJob CRD.
> Installs the RayService CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeRay using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs KubeRay on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay --create-namespace

```

> Installs KubeRay in the 'kuberay' namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay installation
steps.description:
This step checks if KubeRay components are running correctly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

kubectl get crd

```

> Lists all pods in the kuberay namespace to verify they are running.
> Lists all installed Custom Resource Definitions to ensure they are correctly set up.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeRay from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> Uninstalls KubeRay from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your existing resources. This step will upgrade KubeRay to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o yaml > raycluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Back up existing RayCluster configurations.
> Upgrades KubeRay to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o yaml > raycluster-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "RayCluster" in version "kuberay.io/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RayCluster CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means there is an issue with the application code or configuration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kuberay

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable or is overloaded.

**Fix:**
Check the status of your Kubernetes cluster and ensure all nodes are healthy using: kubectl get nodes

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or if the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correct and the cluster is running: kubectl config view

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new Ray features and improved CRD management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeRay pods running in the 'kuberay' namespace, and the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay' should show their status as 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
